title: [OM][Evaluator][domaintool] Drop Kind::Unknown
body:
Due to persistent and fundamental problems with the use of the OM
Evaluator's `Kind::Unknown` value, remove it.  Switch to an unknown bit on
each value to better align with how the OM Evaluator works.

`Kind::Unknown` value doesn't properly work with the evaluator's partial
evaluation.  Whenever a value is partially evaluated it is entered into a
store of objects.  However, using the approach of `Kind::Unknown` means
that this object will later be made invalid.  Instead, an unknown bit can
be set on values when they are known to be unknown (and propagated to
other values).

This is made possible because of the new `om::UnknownValueOp` which can be
used to tie-off ports to be unknown in evaluator-based utilities like
`domaintool`.

Change `domaintool` to Query the class definition to get formal parameter
types and create `UnknownValueOp`s with the correct type instead of using
`AnyType`. This ensures type safety when instantiating classes with
unknown parameters.

AI-assisted-by: Augment (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
